How are the inner planets different from the outer ones?

Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars are the terrestrial planets with solid, rocky surfaces you could walk on. These inner worlds are much smaller than the giant planets and are primarily made of rock and metal.

What is the composition of the gas giants?
Jupiter and Saturn are gas giants, composed mostly of hydrogen and helium with no solid surface to land on. They are like massive, swirling balls of thick atmosphere surrounding a dense, heavy core.

Tell me about the ice giants.

Uranus and Neptune are known as ice giants because they contain more 'ices' like methane and ammonia than the gas giants. These distant worlds are freezing cold and have a distinct bluish color from their atmospheres.

Which planets have rings?

While Saturn is famous for its visible rings, all four of the giant planets in our outer solar system actually possess ring systems. These rings are made of billions of tiny pieces of ice and rock orbiting the planet.

Why is Earth so special?

Earth is a unique terrestrial world because it is the only place in our entire solar system where we have discovered life. Its atmosphere and liquid water make it a perfect oasis in the vastness of space.

The learner previously named the eight planets in order from the Sun. Which of those is the smallest?
That is right; Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and the closest to the Sun. It is only slightly larger than Earth's Moon and has a rocky, cratered surface.

The learner already knows Earth is a terrestrial planet. Does Mars also have a solid surface?
Exactly, Mars is a terrestrial planet because it has a solid, rocky surface just like Earth. It is often called the Red Planet due to the iron minerals in its soil rusting.

Which planet is the largest in our solar system?
Jupiter is the undisputed king of the planets, being the largest world orbiting our Sun. It is so big that all the other planets could fit inside it twice.

Which planet is the most distant from the Sun?
Neptune holds the title of the most distant planet, orbiting far out in the dark reaches of the solar system. It takes about 165 Earth years to complete just one trip around the Sun.

How many planets are officially recognized in the solar system?
There are exactly eight planets in our solar system, ranging from rocky Mercury to the icy giant Neptune. We also recognize five dwarf planets, like Pluto, which are much smaller than the main eight.
